invocation_body: @copilot Can you test the ThemeRegistry and ApolloWrapper and make sure that it works in src/app/providers.tsx?
pr_title: Fix Next.js static export prerendering error, clean up module bundling, and migrate APIs to Firebase Functions
next_human_1_body: @copilot Can you try fixing this, too? 1) Remove unnecessary "use client" from non-React modules Right now you have "use client" in modules that are not components/hooks, which can confuse the bundling graph in strict build pipelines. Do this: Remove "use client" from lib/apollo-client.ts (it’s a factory/util, not a component) Remove "use client" from lib/themes.ts (theme definitions are not React components) Keep "use client" only in: app/providers.tsx lib/ApolloWrapper.tsx lib/ThemeRegistry.tsx lib/ThemeContext.tsx lib/AuthContext.tsx 2) Don’t create singletons at module scope for Apollo You already moved toward a makeApolloClient() factory + useMemo in ApolloWrapper. Make sure you are not still importing a default exported singleton anywhere. Your final shape should be: lib/apollo-client.ts exports makeApolloClient() lib/ApolloWrapper.tsx calls it inside useMemo 3) Make sure your theme registry file that exports ThemeId/defaultThemeId/getThemeById is NOT "use client" Your pasted lib/themes.ts doesn’t match your imports (ThemeId, defaultThemeId, getThemeById). Wherever those actually live, make that file a plain module (no "use client"). Only the context/provider files should be client. 4) Confirm you’re not accidentally triggering static export You are using Firebase Hosting frameworksBackend, which runs a framework build and may behave like “export” in logs. That’s okay—but if you also have output: "export" or a next export step anywhere, it will break SSR features and can amplify these issues. You showed next.config.ts does not set output: "export" (good). 5) Make CI deterministic: use npm ci everywhere In your preview workflow, don’t use npm install. Use: bash npm ci Dependency drift can be the difference between “build passes” and “build crashes”. If you want the fastest confirmation of the exact module causing it Add this temporary step in GitHub Actions right before the build: YAML - name: Debug: show client directives run: | echo "apollo-client.ts:" && sed -n '1,20p' lib/apollo-client.ts echo "themes.ts:" && sed -n '1,20p' lib/themes.ts If either starts with "use client" when it shouldn’t, fix that. If after all that it still fails Then we need the real stack trace, not the “ignore-listed frames”. Do one of these: Run locally with more detail: bash NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1 NODE_OPTIONS="--trace-uncaught" npm run build Or temporarily set in CI: YAML - run: NODE_OPTIONS="--trace-uncaught" npm run build And paste the first ~50 lines of the stack trace that mention your source files. Most likely specific fix for your repo Based on your code and symptoms, the highest-probability fix is: remove "use client" from lib/apollo-client.ts and lib/themes*.ts ensure Apollo client is created inside the wrapper (not module scope) use npm ci in CI If you want, paste your current lib/themes file that actually contains ThemeId/defaultThemeId/getThemeById (path + contents), and I’ll show exactly how to refactor it.
next_human_2_body: @copilot Let's move all of the APIs and convert it - use Firebase Functions, please.
next_human_3_body: @copilot 1. Create a separate Cloud Function for each endpoint. 2. Yes, update all frontend calls to use the Functions URLs, and keep the /api/chat/* paths as rewrites in firebase.json 3. Add to existing functions setup, create a functions directory - I don't have a functions directory right now. Make sure that SSR will still work. 4. Yes. All OpenAI SDK and Firebase Admin SDK should move to functions package.json. 5. Yes. Keep the Secret Manager approach.
